mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lifts equity valuations by lowering discount rates and easing financial conditions, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next 20 trading days when policy is actively easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 3-year sign accuracy falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income growth and slower demand, which should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if, after UNRATE rises by 0.3 pp in 3 months, SPX forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances or the average return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both improve, firms are likely experiencing stronger realized sales with expanding payrolls, which should support earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and rising PAYEMS does not outperform either series alone on 20-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate stays below 55% over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO indicates weakening industrial output and inventory demand, which typically leads equity investors to revise growth expectations downward, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with declining INDPRO produce positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the 5-year rolling correlation between INDPRO changes and forward returns is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A fast CPIAUCSL inflation print combined with a low FEDFUNDS rate implies the Fed is behind the inflation curve, so discount-rate repricing and multiple compression should push the S&P 500 lower over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 2 percentage points on a 12-month basis
regime_hint: inflation shock with negative real policy rates
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-above-policy-rate regime does not produce below-zero 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the interaction term is not negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in HOUST signals stronger housing demand and construction activity, which supports cyclical growth expectations and should lift the S&P 500 over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month increases in HOUST are not followed by positive average 60-day SPX returns, or if the triggered hit rate falls below 53% across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE marks a genuine labor-market deterioration, which should weaken consumer spending expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-down and UNRATE-up state does not yield negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if it underperforms a simple UNRATE-only rule out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDPC1 growth with firm PAYEMS growth indicates broad demand strength, which should support corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth exceeds its prior quarter while PAYEMS is rising year over year
failure_condition: Reject if the combined GDP-and-payroll expansion regime does not outperform the unconditional 60-day SPX baseline, or if triggered instances fail to deliver positive returns more than 50% of the time.
